seo.title: GitHub Actions vs GitLab CI vs Vercel
seo.description: A practical comparison of GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Vercel for CI/CD for AI Apps.

content.intro: Comparison of CI/CD tools and practices for AI application deployment, focusing on implementation trade-offs for DevOps engineers and AI developers

options.name: GitHub Actions
options.tagline: Integrated workflow automation with GitHub ecosystem
options.best_for: Teams already using GitHub with moderate CI/CD complexity

options.name: GitLab CI
options.tagline: All-in-one DevOps platform with built-in CI/CD
options.best_for: Organizations prioritizing unified development and deployment workflows

options.name: Vercel
options.tagline: Static site deployment with AI-specific optimizations
options.best_for: Frontend-focused AI applications with frequent preview deployments

options.name: ArgoCD
options.best_for: Teams requiring declarative model deployment pipelines

options.name: Docker
options.tagline: Containerization for consistent AI runtime environments

criteria.name: Implementation Effort
criteria.description: Complexity of setting up AI-specific pipeline components

criteria.name: Cost Profile
criteria.description: Expense of running AI evaluation suites and deployments

values.GitHub Actions: Variable (free tier limited, paid plans for minutes)

criteria.name: AI Testing Support
criteria.description: Built-in capabilities for LLM output validation

criteria.name: Model Deployment
criteria.description: Tooling for AI model versioning and rollback

criteria.name: Preview Deployments
criteria.description: Support for testing AI features in staging environments

verdict.summary: GitHub Actions and GitLab CI offer balanced trade-offs for general AI CI/CD needs, while Vercel excels in frontend deployment scenarios. ArgoCD and Docker provide stronger infrastructure control but require more implementation effort. The best choice depends on existing tooling ecosystems and specific AI workflow requirements.

use_cases.scenario: Small AI teams needing quick setup
use_cases.reason: Integrated platform reduces setup complexity for basic AI deployment needs

use_cases.scenario: Large-scale model deployment with rollback requirements
use_cases.reason: Declarative Kubernetes management enables reliable model version control

use_cases.scenario: Frontend-focused AI application with frequent previews
use_cases.reason: Built-in preview deployments simplify testing of LLM-powered features
